Present: Mr. Justice Mohammad Karim Khan Agha Mr. fustice Abdul Mobeen Lakho Appellants: The State Complainant: Date of hearing: Date of announcement: Arif Abbasi, Faizan Abbasi and Adil Abbasi through Mr. Ajab Khan Khattak advocate Mr. Muhammad lqbal Awan Deputy Prosecutor General Sindh Shabbir Ahmed Khan through Mr. Mamoon A.K.Sherwany advocate 08.M.2021, 1.4.M.2021. 5 IUDGMENT Mohammad Karim Khan t.- Arif Abbasi, Farzan Abbasi and Adil Abbasi appellants were tried by leamed Anti-Terrorism Court-XVI, Karachi. Alter fuIl-dressed trial, appellants were convicted under section 302(b) PPC for causing qatl-e-amd of deceased Sodheer Ahmed, Wazeer Ahmed and Muhammad Nawaz and sentenced to rigorous lire imprisonment with direction to pay fine of Rs.10o000/-each and in case of failure, to surfer s.I for six months more. Appellants were also ordered to pay compensation of Rs.10o000/- each to the legal heirs of deceased as provided under section 544-A Cr.p.C and in default, they were ordered to suffer s.I for six months more. Appellant Arif Abbasi was also convicted under section 23(r)(a) of sindh Arms Act, 2013 ard sentenced to suffer s.I for five years and to pay fine of Rs.10,000/-. In case of default in payment of fine, appellant was ordered to suffer sI for three months more. All the sentences were directed to run concurrently and the appellanE were also extended benefit of secrion 382-8 Cr.p.C. 2. Precisely the facts as disclosed by complainant Shabbir Ahmed Khan in FIR No.92/201.5 registered at p.S paposh Nagar, Karachi for offences under sections 302/34 ppC read with section 7 of Anti-Terrorismt

All the sentences were directed to run concurrently and the appellanE were also extended benefit of secrion 382-8 Cr.p.C. 2. Precisely the facts as disclosed by complainant Shabbir Ahmed Khan in FIR No.92/201.5 registered at p.S paposh Nagar, Karachi for offences under sections 302/34 ppC read with section 7 of Anti-Terrorismt 323 L 4ct,7997 are that on 31.05.2015, he was available at his hotel along with his brothers namely sodheer Ahmed and wazeer Ahmed as wel as his brother-in-law namely Muhammad Nawaz when at about 2215 hours, accused Faisal @ Mota, Arif, Faizan and Adil duly armed with weapons came there and fired upon them. Due to such firing his brothers as well as his brother-in-law received fue arm injuries. Thereafter, accused r.rn away from the place of occurrence. Injured were brought at the Abbasi shaheed Hospital where brothers of the complainant Sudheer and Wazeer were found expired whereas his brother-in-law expired during treatment after 1'-llzhour. After registration of the aforesaid FIR, the investigation was carried out by Inspector Ali Hyder shah. Appellants were arrested on 01'.06.2015 and on the pointation of accused Arif Abbasi, police recovered crime weapon i.e. pistol from his house. FIR No. 97/201.5 under Section 23(a) of Sindh Arms Act 20L3 was registered at p.S paposh Nagar against accused Arif Abbasi on behalf of State. 3. After conclusion of investigation, appellants were challaned to the Anti-Terrorism Court whereas, accused Faisal Abbasi @ Mota was declared proclaimed offender and proceedings under Section 8Z/8g Cr.P.C were initiated against him. 4. Upon indictment, accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. At trial, the prosecution examined L0 prosecution witnesses, who produced and exhibited the relevant documents before the learned trial Court, Thereafter, prosecution side was closed, 5. Statements of accused were recorded under section 342, Cr.p.C. n which they have denied the allegations and professed their innocence.

At trial, the prosecution examined L0 prosecution witnesses, who produced and exhibited the relevant documents before the learned trial Court, Thereafter, prosecution side was closed, 5. Statements of accused were recorded under section 342, Cr.p.C. n which they have denied the allegations and professed their innocence. They have neither examined themselves on oath nor produced any witness in their defence. 6. After hearing the Iearned counsel for the parties, learned trial court convicted and sentenced the appellants as mentioned earlier in this judgment. 7. During pendency of the titled appeals, the appellants and the Iegal heirs of the deceased filed application for extending permission with respect to effect compromise between the parties. Vide order dated 2 11 t 38q --- I '11'.72.2020, the compromise application was sent to learned trial court for conducting entire due proceedings and submitting report regarding genuineness of the compromise.

Findincs:- In uictu of the reports of Mukhtiarkar, NADRA, IO/PI Ali Haider Shah, noticey published in Daily ^lsws?apers "Autami Awaz", "Daily lang" and "The Nation" so also statements of legal heirs the number aid authenticity of legal hcirs is established; no othq person has appeared fn objection; the statements and ampromise applications hooe also disclosed all the legal heirs including minors; the statements of all adult tcgal heirs of three deceaed confirm that compromise entcred into respectiaely and collectioely by eath and all of them is genuinz, free from pressure, coercion and,/or any intimidation; the nght of Qisas has been waiaed by each of them and all of them so also on behalf of minor legal heirs- by tluir respecthte mothers (uridows), no compensation has been claimed by any of the legal luirs, houet)el ight of Diyat of minor cannot be waiaed as pir *ction 309(b) PPC." / 8. After receipt of the above report, on 18.02.2071, the Nazir of this Court was directed to determine the due share of compensation/diyat ol the minors and to submit such report. In compliance of such directions, the Nazir submitted his report dated 25.08.2021 wherein submitted that total share of minors which came to Rs.42,38356/- beng diyat amount calculated as per Notificatioru Today counsel for the appellants submitted that total amount of Rs.42,38,356/- as per Nazir's Report has been paid and in lieu thereof produced receipt of the Nazir. 9. Since the appellants have deposited the share of diyat amount as calculated by the Nazir and leamed counsel for the complainant as well as learned DPG are satisfied that all the legal requirements are complied with and raised no objection for allowing compromise application as prayed. 10. Since, interest of minors stands safeguarded; therefore, there will be no impediment to allow the compromise with the appellants. As, the report furnished by the trial court reveals that compromise between the parties appears to be voluntary, genuine and without any duress a"rl I 3 ?-t I, c@rcion, therefore, permission to compound the offence is accorded to the parties and as a result whereoi compromise between the parties is hereby accepted and appellants Arif Abbasi, Faizan Abbasi and AdiI Abbasi are acquitted of the charges under 5.302 (b) PPC in terms compromise. Nazir is directed to deposit the Diyat amount of the minors in any Government profitable scheme, which shall be payable to them as and when they may attain majori$r. 11. For the foregoing reasons, Special Criminal Anti-Terrorism Appeal No. 156 of 2019 stands disposed of in the above terms. 12. As regards to the Special Criminal Anti-Terrorism Appeal No. 157 of 2019 is concemed, the same is preferred by appellant Arif Abbasi against his conviction and sentence under section 23(a) Sindh Arms 4ct,2013, which is a non-compoundable offence, hence, instant appeal is being decided on merits to the extent of said offence. 13.

For the foregoing reasons, Special Criminal Anti-Terrorism Appeal No. 156 of 2019 stands disposed of in the above terms. 12. As regards to the Special Criminal Anti-Terrorism Appeal No. 157 of 2019 is concemed, the same is preferred by appellant Arif Abbasi against his conviction and sentence under section 23(a) Sindh Arms 4ct,2013, which is a non-compoundable offence, hence, instant appeal is being decided on merits to the extent of said offence. 13. We have gone through the proseortion evidence, which transpires that prosecution has proved its' case under Section 23(a) of Sindh Arms Act 2013 against the appellant Arif Abbasi through cogent and confidence inspiring evidence. Therefore, we hold that prosecution has proved its' case beyond any shadow of reasonable doubt against appellant Arif Abbasi. However, at this juncture, leamed counsel for the appellant has submitted that appellant has served out his sentence therefore, he may be released. On perusal of record it transpires that appellant Arif Abbasi was convicted under Section 23(a) of Sindh Arms Act 2013 and was sentenced to suffer S.I for five years and to pay fine of Rs.10,000/- and in default, he was ordered to suffer SI for tfuee months more. We have gone through ]ail Roll dated 19.09.2020, which reflects that appellant has served his sentence including remission upto 28.09.2020 as 05 yeats, 06 months and 13 days, leamed trid court had ordered that the sentences run concurrently, therefore, we are in agreement with the learned counsel for the appellant Arif Abbasi, that he has served out his sentence in the above referred offence. 74. In the view of above, Special Criminal Anti-Terrorism Appeal No.757/20L9 is dismissed, however, Superintendent, Central Prison 2 f" 3q'e /t Karachi shall release the appellant Arif Abbasi, if he is not required in any other custody case as he has already served out his sentence for his conviction under the Sindh Arms Act. 15 The above appeals stand disposed of in the above terms J JUDGE F
